question.text: In an InvIT IPO, strategic investor(s) shall invest not less than ___% and not more than ___% of the total offer size.

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under InvIT Regulations, strategic investor(s) shall, either jointly or severally, invest not less than 5% and not more than 25% of the total offer size and shall enter into binding unit subscription agreement with the Investment Manager. **The Trap:** 25% is also the maximum subscription from any single investor, but the strategic investor minimum is 5%, not 10%. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Strategic investors (like anchor investors in equity IPOs) provide price discovery and confidence to retail investors by committing 5-25% of the offer size.
